title: audit — compliance evidence
description: Primary control points for GDPR (Art. 5, 17, 30), SOX §404, HIPAA §164.312(b), PCI-DSS Requirement 10, ISO 27001 A.12.4, SOC 2 CC7.1, and CCPA §1798.185. Audit is the AUTHORITATIVE compliance evidence surface — regulators, DPOs, and forensic investigators consume this module.

controls.evidence: Every mutating operation writes an immutable audit row with field-level diff. The vendor observer captures create/update/delete/restored automatically. Row is immutable after write (AuditObserver::updating throws NOTIFICATIONS_AUDIT_ROW_IMMUTABLE). Delete is refused except via PurgeExpiredAuditsJob service account (AuditObserver::deleting). Route-level middleware audit.enforce_read_only refuses non-GET methods.

7-year default retention balances 'no longer than necessary' against regulator/audit requirements. Enterprise tenants may extend to 10 years via AuditRetentionPolicy with an audit_retention_extended entitlement + a compliance-officer-signed override_reason. PurgeExpiredAuditsJob runs nightly per-tenant honoring the active policy.

compliance::UserErased cascade invokes AuditRedactionListener (owned here, subscribed via listeners.json) — REDACTS old_values/new_values fields matching the erased subject's PII to a SHA-256 hash. Fact-of-mutation record (event, auditable_type, auditable_id, created_at) IS PRESERVED — regulators may need proof of the erasure years later. Full-row deletion is NEVER performed on subject erasure (that would violate Art. 30 records-of-processing requirement).

Every audit row IS a record of a processing activity. The `audits` table with 7y retention is the platform's primary Art. 30 evidence. Per-controller record-of-processing exports available via /api/v1/audits/export?kind=regulator (Enterprise + audit_regulator_export entitlement).

old_values + new_values encrypted at rest via Postgres TDE. audit_retention_policies.override_reason (contains legal / compliance context) also encrypted. Every retention-policy CRUD writes an audit row (via the vendor Auditable trait on the policy itself — self-auditing). PurgeExpiredAuditsJob writes a summary meta-audit ('audit.purged') so the trail records itself being purged.
Audit reveals actor + time + affected records for breach investigation. AuditQuery.byUser + byEntity + bySince/until supports the 72h breach-notification window.

Financial mutation records retained 7 years by default (SOX baseline). Every finance-module mutation (Membership, TenantSubscription, Pass, Transaction) writes a compliance-grade audit row. Retention extension to 10 years available via AuditRetentionPolicy for enterprises operating in SOX-regulated markets.
CFO / compliance-officer role has audit.audits.viewAny + audit.audits.reveal_raw_diff permissions. Chain-of-custody row written on every raw-diff reveal (meta-audit).

Every mutation of PHI-adjacent records (Athlete, AthleteGuardian, Consent, HealthNote — planned Wave 7) writes a compliance-grade audit row. Access-only events register as custom audit events ('read.phi_record'). Retention typically extended to 10 years via AuditRetentionPolicy for HIPAA-covered entities.
6-year minimum retention — our 7-year default exceeds. HIPAA-covered tenants may need to raise to 10y via audit_retention_extended entitlement (regulator requirement varies by state).

controls.principle: audit trails for all access to cardholder data
controls.evidence: Every mutation of finance-module cardholder-data-adjacent records writes an audit row. Read events register as custom audit events ('read.cardholder_data'). The vendor Auditable trait supports registerAuditableTransition to add non-mutation events like reads.

controls.principle: audit trails secured to prevent unauthorized modification
controls.evidence: Three-layer immutability: (a) DB triggers refuse UPDATE + DELETE on `audits` outside a session tagged with the purge service actor; (b) AuditObserver::updating throws NOTIFICATIONS_AUDIT_ROW_IMMUTABLE; (c) audit.enforce_read_only middleware refuses non-GET methods.
1-year minimum, 3 months hot — our 7y default + 90d hot exceeds. Enterprise 10y available via AuditRetentionPolicy.
The `audits` table IS the event log. Vendor `AuditableObserver` fires on every mutation. Our AuditObserver enforces required columns + immutability + non-null tenant_id.
old_values + new_values encrypted at rest. Route-level read-only middleware. Immutability enforced by observer + DB triggers. Chain-of-custody meta-audits on every raw-diff reveal.
user_type + user_id polymorphic actor on every audit row. Platform-admin actions carry PlatformUser::class as user_type. Service-account actions carry ServiceAccount::class.

AuditQuery surface supports the anomaly-detection queries (unusual actor volume, unusual mutation rate per tenant, off-hours access). Monitoring metrics (academorix.audit.records_total, records_by_event, records_by_actor) feed the Grafana anomaly dashboard.
AuditQuery.byUser + byEntity + bySince/until provides the fact-finding surface for incident responders. Cross-tenant queries via platform-admin surface for support/on-call.
Every config change on AuditRetentionPolicy writes an audit row (via HasAuditable on the policy). Retention_years changes + override_ends_at changes + override_reason changes tracked.
Every DSAR request writes an audit row. CCPA response actions (delete, correction, opt-out) generate audit rows via their handling modules. 24-month minimum retention — our 7y default exceeds.
AuditRedactionListener redacts PII in old_values + new_values on UserErased cascade. Fact-of-mutation record preserved (see GDPR Art. 17 above).
